name: Lorca and Guadalentín River Circuit
name_original: Lorca — Paseo circular por el Río Guadalentín
description: This easy 3.8-mile (6.1 km) circular hike along the Guadalentín River in Lorca is perfect for all fitness levels, taking about 1 hour and 44
region_intro: Hiking around Lorca offers diverse and rugged landscapes within Spain's Murcia region. The city is situated in the Guadalentín Valley, characterized by intricate terrain and surrounded by mountains. Lorca's municipality extends to a coastline, including the Parque Regional de Cabo Cope - Puntas de Calnegre, providing varied coastal hiking opportunities. The region features a mix of valley paths, mountainous backdrops, and coastal trails. Best hiking trails around Lorca The most popular hiking route is Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23), a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 14 minutes to complete. This easy coastal path is suitable for any fitness level. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lorca — Circular walk along the Guadalentin River, an easy 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This route follows the Guadalentín River, offering a gentle walk. Local hikers also love the The Cerro de la Selva Trail (PR-MU 72) — Tour from Zarzadilla de Totana, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail leading through the Cerro de la Selva area, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Lorca is defined by the Guadalentín Valley, mountainous backdrops, and coastal areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy river walks to more challenging mountain routes. The routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Lorca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Lorca?
A: Lorca offers a wide selection of nearly 90 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes 29 easy routes, 55 moderate options, and 5 more challenging paths.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Lorca?
A: Hiking in Lorca provides a diverse experience, from the intricate and rugged terrain of the Guadalentín Valley to mountainous backdrops and coastal areas. The region extends to the Parque Regional de Cabo Cope - Puntas de Calnegre, offering trails along beaches like Puntas de Calnegre and Cala Honda.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Lorca?
A: Yes, Lorca has 29 easy trails. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899539531/tour-along-the-cope-marina-trail-sl-mu-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)&lt;/a&gt;, an easy coastal path suitable for any fitness level. Another gentle choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899341713/lorca-circular-walk-along-the-guadalentin-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca — Circular walk along the Guadalentin River&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the river for a pleasant stroll.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Lorca?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For an easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899341713/lorca-circular-walk-along-the-guadalentin-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca — Circular walk along the Guadalentin River&lt;/a&gt;. A moderate circular hike that combines urban and historical sights is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guerra Theatre – Church of St. Matthew loop from Lorca Sutullena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see on hikes around Lorca?
A: Lorca's trails can lead you to fascinating natural and historical sites. You might explore coastal caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/998553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of Wolves&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1589582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de las Cuevas de la Encarnación and Cueva del Rey Moro&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features scenic mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3579469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucecicas&lt;/a&gt;, offering impressive views.

Q: Are there any trails that go through mountainous areas?
A: Absolutely. Lorca is nestled among mountains, providing many routes with elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e896123013/the-cerro-de-la-selva-trail-pr-mu-72-tour-from-zarzadilla-de-totana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Cerro de la Selva Trail (PR-MU 72) — Tour from Zarzadilla de Totana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.3 km trail that takes you through the Cerro de la Selva area, offering a more challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lorca?
A: The hiking routes in Lorca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from gentle river walks to more challenging mountain routes, and the beautiful natural scenery of the Guadalentín Valley and coastal areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lorca?
A: Lorca, being in Spain's Murcia region, generally offers good hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, especially in the valley, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, or focusing on coastal routes where sea breezes might offer some relief.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the coast or beaches?
A: Yes, Lorca's municipality extends to a coastline, including the Parque Regional de Cabo Cope - Puntas de Calnegre. Trails in this area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899539531/tour-along-the-cope-marina-trail-sl-mu-23" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour along the Cope Marina Trail (SL-MU 23)&lt;/a&gt;, provide scenic views of the Mediterranean and access to beaches like Puntas de Calnegre and Cala Honda.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for spotting local flora and fauna?
A: Many routes traverse areas known for their abundant flora and fauna, especially within the Guadalentín Valley and the surrounding mountainous regions. The Espartaria Route, mentioned in regional research, is a longer trail specifically designed to showcase the region's rich natural life, offering opportunities to observe local biodiversity.

Q: Are there any shorter, easy circular walks available?
A: For a shorter, easy circular walk, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17267422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Trees loop from El Zorro Swimming Pool&lt;/a&gt;. This route is just over 4 km long and offers a pleasant, accessible option for a quick outing.
tips: It is a fortress of Arab origin, although over time it has become a symbol of the defensive border architecture of medieval times. Lorca's castle is elongated and its plan is polygonal. One of the most representative aspects of this construction are its towers: the Torre del Espolón and the Torre Alfonsina.

Lorca is a name that pulsates with history, and you feel it most vividly here on the square. A historic city center that takes you through medieval streets, baroque palaces, and churches that will take your breath away.

S. XVII - XVIII. The House of Guevara or House of the Columns is the most significant private building of all the civil baroque of the city. In 1689 the knight of the order of Santiago, Juan de Guevara García de Alcaraz, belonging to the Lorca oligarchy, decided to finish it for housing. The portal, completed in 1694, was designed as an altarpiece with Solomonic columns,

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular walk?
faqs.answer: The path along the Guadalentín River offers a varied surface. Approximately 4.05 km are paved, with about 662 meters unpaved, and some sections of unknown surface. You'll encounter a mix of road, path, and dedicated hiking path, all generally easy to navigate.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: While the walk primarily follows the river, the broader Lorca area is rich in history. You can find nearby historical sites such as the Colegiata de San Patricio, Plaza de España and Collegiate Church of San Patricio, Lorca, and the Guevara Palace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular walk is classified as an easy hike, making it great for any fitness level and suitable for all skill levels. Its accessible paths make it a good choice for families.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Guadalentín Valley is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in the Murcia region. Consider avoiding the peak heat of summer afternoons.

faqs.question: Is the Lorca — Circular walk along the Guadalentin River dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near roads, and remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and is the trailhead accessible by public transport?
faqs.answer: The starting point of this circular walk is accessible by public transport. Specific parking information isn't detailed, but as a popular local route, parking options are typically available nearby. We recommend checking local maps for designated parking areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to enjoy the Lorca — Circular walk along the Guadalentin River. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the Guadalentín River?
faqs.answer: The Guadalentín River is known for its environmental interest as a habitat for various water birds and birds of prey. Keep an eye out for species like the black-winged stilt, among others, especially near the riverbanks.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Transmurciana 10, Camino del Alto Guadalentín, and Camino del Río Guadalentín, among others.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the walk is located in Lorca, a city with amenities, you'll find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the vicinity to enjoy before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this easy walk?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty and relatively short length, comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are usually sufficient. A camera might be nice for capturing the river scenery.

waypoints.name: Plaza de España and Collegiate Church of San Patricio, Lorca
